review_notes: The functions provided are generally well-aligned with the HIPAA control for protection against malicious software. They map to relevant Kubernetes security features that help mitigate the risk of malicious software. However, while the function names are mostly accurate, there is room for slight improvement in clarity and specificity.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_admission_controller_pod_security_enabled' to 'k8s_admission_controller_security_policy_enforced' for more clarity on the action being enforced.
title: 164_308_a_5_ii_b: 164.308(a)(5)(ii)(B) Protection from malicious software
step1_reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.308(a)(5)(ii)(B) focuses on protection from malicious software, which can be mapped to several Kubernetes security practices. Ensuring that images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment (k8s_image_vulnerability_scanning_enabled) helps prevent malicious software from being introduced. Admission controllers can enforce security policies to prevent the deployment of potentially harmful pods (k8s_admission_controller_pod_security_enabled). Applying restrictive pod security standards (k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted) can limit the capabilities of pods, reducing the risk of malicious activity. Network policies that deny ingress by default (k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_ingress) can prevent unauthorized access, further protecting against malicious software.
